steps.text: The y-intercept of a graph is the point where $x = 0$. Here, $x$ is seconds after the marble started moving and $y$ is the marble's height in inches. So the y-intercept is the height at the moment the marble started moving ($x = 0$).
Reading the graph at $x = 0$: the height is $y = 8$ inches. So the y-intercept is the point $(0, 8)$.
The correct interpretation must convey that the height was 8 inches at the start (when $x = 0$), not a minimum, and not at a later time.

• Choice A: "The marble's height was 0 inches above the ground 8 seconds after it started moving." This describes an x-intercept, not the y-intercept, and swaps the values. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: "The marble's height was 8 inches above the ground when it started moving." This correctly identifies the y-intercept: at $x = 0$, $y = 8$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: "The marble's minimum height was 0 inches above the ground." The y-intercept is not about minimum height. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: "The marble's minimum height was 8 inches above the ground." The y-intercept describes the starting height, not the minimum. Eliminated.
